speaker: MATT
raw_text: Travis is going to get them. Wow. Two weeks to stew on this and it's already falling apart.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Wade the waters.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Exactly. But I'll be doing signings, panels, and meeting a bunch of you Critters that are out in that area, so come by and say hi this weekend. And if not, go say hi to Steve and Mary, wherever that happens, in the lesser part of Dallas. I don't know if it is, I just like--

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Yeah, there's a lesser part of Dallas, totally, over with the lesser-- are you guys throwing up because of how nervous you are?

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: What? No. Oh, my dice! Where are my dice?!

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Where are your dice?

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: I'm so nervous, I don't even have my dice!

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: I'll go find them, I'll go find them.

speaker: MATT
raw_text: Possibly. This is very true. So look for that tomorrow on geekandsundry.com, that'll be fun and exciting. Also, for those who didn't catch it yesterday, we had an awesome event at Meltdown Comics with the official Dungeons & Dragons crew announcing: one, their new storyline, which is the Storm King's Thunder, I think is what it's called. Really awesome, bringing the giants back into the world with a very fleshed-out story. I'm super excited about it, and keep tabs on that whenever that comes out, should be in the next few months, I think. I've seen some stuff behind-the-scenes and it looks really, really cool. But as part of that event yesterday, we also kicked off and announced a new video series campaign that I'm doing with the actual Wizards of the Coast Dungeons & Dragons team. Me and Chris Perkins wrote and designed together a story that I fleshed out and am running for a series of celebrity guests, one of which is our fantastic Ashley Johnson there.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Travis is going to get them. Wow. Two weeks to stew on this and it's already falling apart.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: This is the exact icy, steely demeanor that I depend on.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Oh my god, are they in there?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: While he looks for those--

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: I have extras, if you need some.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: I do want to also say the newest Dear Abby article series for Critical Role characters went up-- or goes up tomorrow, right?

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Tomorrow, Fridays.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Friday. And whose is that one?

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Who goes up next?

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: What? Who goes up what next?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: The next Dear Abby?

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Oh, I think it's me!

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Oh! Then we'll have Vex tomorrow coming up. See her answering questions to various distraught people throughout the realms of Exandria, and her I'm sure very astute--

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: I'm a really good giver of advice. I learned that about myself.

preceding_turns.speaker: ASHLEY
preceding_turns.text: It's true.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: More about quantity than quality? Maybe?

speaker: MATT
raw_text: That's right, we're waiting on Scanlan to get here. Other guests include Utkarsh, Shelby, Jonah-- I wrote all the first names down because the last names, some of them were hard to pronounce, but you can look up the actual announcements to get the full names. I'm really bad at this. It's been two weeks, give me a break.

preceding_turns.speaker: ASHLEY
preceding_turns.text: Hey!

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: You're a celebrity!

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: That was pretty great, guys. I'm not going to lie, that was pretty legit.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Just keep vamping. Keep vamping until the bard gets here.

speaker: MATT
raw_text: No, it's two weeks from now.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: E3's next week!

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: E3 is next week.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Oh wait, no it's not. It's two weeks from now.

speaker: MATT
raw_text: So. Continuing where we left off two weeks ago. The intrepid party of adventurers known as Vox Machina, after recently freeing the town of Westruun from being occupied and ruled by the roving Herd of the Storm, which Grog once hailed from, cut down their brutal leader Kevdak and temporarily restored power to a combination of himself and Kevdak's son, Zanror. An accord was made between the two of you to agree to go after and destroy the ancient black dragon Umbrasyl, a member of the Chroma Conclave, and marked for you to eventually destroy anyway, but who had currently made a deal with the Herd in exchange for goods, gold, and ruling over the town, would let them live and be essentially undisturbed, seemingly. You gathered an army, you forged a plan, and an ambush was set for the next time the dragon swooped down from atop the mountain Gatshadow to retrieve its package. At this time, the trap was sprung, the dragon held fast by hooks and chains, a giant metallic trap designed and orchestrated by Percival, and everything was going great. The dragon had sustained quite a bit of damage and seemed to be in a rough place until it broke free and left much of the Herd fallen, dissolving and screaming. Also, Scanlan and Vax Dimension Door'd into the inside of the dragon and have spent a number of rounds now deciding how to get out of it maybe?

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: E3 is two weeks from now, and I'm also going to be at Amazing Comic Con Vegas two weeks from now. And Signal Boost is currently in its second week, which is hilarious, and amazing, and you should totally check it out because it's weird.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Yeah. You're like the most interesting man in the world.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: Thank you.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: It's fantastic.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: I've always thought that about you, Taliesin.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: Oh. Thanks. I'm now... yeah. I'm going to die today, thank you. (laughter)

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: No you're not!

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Pike's got us covered. Right, Pike?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: All right, great. So, without further ado, and no further delaying the inevitable--

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: You got a little something on your--

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Little thing on my nose, right there?

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: You might need to go the bathroom, check it out.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: No, it's great. So let's go ahead and roll the intro and start playing Critical Role. [dramatic music]

speaker: MATT
raw_text: So. Continuing where we left off two weeks ago. The intrepid party of adventurers known as Vox Machina, after recently freeing the town of Westruun from being occupied and ruled by the roving Herd of the Storm, which Grog once hailed from, cut down their brutal leader Kevdak and temporarily restored power to a combination of himself and Kevdak's son, Zanror. An accord was made between the two of you to agree to go after and destroy the ancient black dragon Umbrasyl, a member of the Chroma Conclave, and marked for you to eventually destroy anyway, but who had currently made a deal with the Herd in exchange for goods, gold, and ruling over the town, would let them live and be essentially undisturbed, seemingly. You gathered an army, you forged a plan, and an ambush was set for the next time the dragon swooped down from atop the mountain Gatshadow to retrieve its package. At this time, the trap was sprung, the dragon held fast by hooks and chains, a giant metallic trap designed and orchestrated by Percival, and everything was going great. The dragon had sustained quite a bit of damage and seemed to be in a rough place until it broke free and left much of the Herd fallen, dissolving and screaming. Also, Scanlan and Vax Dimension Door'd into the inside of the dragon and have spent a number of rounds now deciding how to get out of it maybe?

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: E3 is two weeks from now, and I'm also going to be at Amazing Comic Con Vegas two weeks from now. And Signal Boost is currently in its second week, which is hilarious, and amazing, and you should totally check it out because it's weird.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Yeah. You're like the most interesting man in the world.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: Thank you.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: It's fantastic.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: I've always thought that about you, Taliesin.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: Oh. Thanks. I'm now... yeah. I'm going to die today, thank you. (laughter)

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: No you're not!

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Pike's got us covered. Right, Pike?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: All right, great. So, without further ado, and no further delaying the inevitable--

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: You got a little something on your--

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Little thing on my nose, right there?

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: You might need to go the bathroom, check it out.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: No, it's great. So let's go ahead and roll the intro and start playing Critical Role. [dramatic music]

speaker: MATT
raw_text: You can see it curving up ahead of you and with how high the sun is in the sky, it's around noon, there's a heavy bit of shadow, and at this distance? Make a perception check.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Can't hear you.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: So. We had two 13s and a--

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: 12. All right. So Grog, Vax, what are you guys doing?

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: I'm taking--

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: The Bramblewood is now beneath you, you're heading towards the mountain, you can see the edge of the forest now.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Maybe I go first, just in case something--

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Yeah, go for it.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: The Bramblewood? I'm holding on, right? With the Titanstone Knuckles. Whereabouts is the axe lodged? Can I see where the chain is attached to?

speaker: MATT
raw_text: Okay, we'll get to that in just a minute. As it is-- the dragon's speed is it can fly 80 feet per round.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: I do?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Because you're currently holding onto the chain, but I believe you have advantage on them because you are a barbarian.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: I have Danger Sense. So just one roll?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Yeah. So just roll.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: That's not great. 11.

preceding_turns.speaker: ASHLEY
preceding_turns.text: Can I-- do I see them?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: No! They are way over-- they're like a tiny little dragon speck in the distance. We'll get to you guys here in just a moment, but their situation is a little more dire and immediate, so I have to figure out what's going on with them right now.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: What if we could've chased after them? Right away?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: You can if you want to. Did you want to go after them?

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Yeah.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: I would have chased after them, immediately, when it took off.

speaker: MATT
raw_text: Yeah, imagine that! At this point, now, as this has gone on for a little while as you guys began to ascend and it went upward, it's now partway up the mountainside. You can see the forest has gotten more sparse. There are trees scattered across the top of the mountaintop.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Are you raging?

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Yeah.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: I need you to go ahead and make a dexterity saving throw.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Which I have advantage on. 18.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: 18. You take 15 points of bludgeoning damage, which is reduced by half, so you're only taking seven. However, you are knocked prone.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: (laughing) On his back.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Yes, which causes you to begin tumbling off the back of the tail. The wind of it-- (thump thump)

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Shit!

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: The chain of returning is still attached to you, however, and as you go tumbling off the back of the tail, it catches you, and you are now swinging towards the back of the creature, of the dragon now, back where you started. Only the dragon is very aware of your presence. But you didn't plummet to your death!

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: That's good!

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: That was the end of your turn. Now it's the top of Umbrasyl's turn.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: These dragons are hard to kill!

speaker: MATT
raw_text: Okay. You're in a weird position now where the blade's at your side, so pulling away from the body, there's very little strength to pull that way, since you punched through the wound. So you're essentially holding the blade right here.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Might as well say something cool. I can do this all day! I'm really not confident.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: All right. Vax.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: I would like to use my movement, if I can, with the blade I used to go this way, and I cut a big foot-long thing. I want to try to use my movement to reverse the direction of the blade and stab inward.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: It would be part of the attack to draw it out and attack. It wouldn't use your movement. It would be part of the attack to draw it out and attack with it. It wouldn't use your movement.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: My dagger hand's the one that's out. I want to try to maneuver the sword around and stab inward and keep cutting bacon in here. I want to attack the dragon, but still from the inside with the hand that's inside.

speaker: MATT
raw_text: The dragon's moved ahead to this point now since you guys have fallen off.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: That is ten points of damage with poison on it. It's a DC of 15 for poison. I'm sure it makes it.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Yeah, it makes it. It would've had to roll a one to not make it, unfortunately.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Could I add one bonus thing at the end of mine?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Sure.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Can I see Scanlan and Bigby's Hand at all? Or is the world just a clusterfuck?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Make a perception check, just glancing around you.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Four.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Four. No. You see earth beneath you, sky above, mountain to your left, and the inevitable plummet coming at you.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: I'm thinking about my mother as I fall backward through space.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Distance between me and them?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Vax is about 20 feet from you, Grog's about 60, 70 feet from you.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: What's the range of-- shit.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: 50 feet.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Remember, we're going to be going the same direction.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: They're both falling, yeah. Vax will hit the ground first.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Vax will?

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: I'm higher.

speaker: MATT
raw_text: Okay. The hand comes forward, sends out this cupping motion, the fingers below. You, eyes closed, clenching tight, (impact). All of a sudden, the wind stops, and it's like you're being held by your mother again, a memory you haven't recalled since you were a child. You open your eyes slowly, and you're still above the ground! Holy shit! But there's a giant purple hand around you and a very freaked out Scanlan clutching the outside.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: You're doing very well. You're doing very well.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Wow. This didn't go well.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: I'm hoping you can hold on for a minute.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Okay. Yeah! I'm going to drop Bigby's-- 60 feet is not plummeting speed? I can't catch up to them as they fall?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Right now, you could catch up to one of them.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: All right. Because Grog is over me, right?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Yeah, Grog is in an arc, so you would have to meet him as he fell.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: I'll try to catch him then.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Okay, so you clutch onto Bigby's Hand and move as he plummets.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Shit, wait. Wait.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: What are you doing? What are you doing, Scanlan?

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Shit. Vax is below me, though.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: He said you can get to one of them, so it's just a Sophie's Choice.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: It's a Sophie's Choice.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: But who's on the lowest hit points?

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Yeah, I would go for Grog if he's above me, with the hand.

speaker: MATT
raw_text: You do, actually. Not very well. Shale has been around longer than you have as part of the herd, but is mostly considered past her prime as a warrior. Since she lost her arm, was set to the side, and a lot of the herd seems to not value their elders when they become less powerful in battle, but even still, she carries a bit of spit and vinegar to her. But it's been a while since you've seen her, and to see her surviving out here, both curious and honorable.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Welcome, Chris, welcome!

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: Is he coming to this side?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Right to the edge, next to you, Keyleth.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Do you know how to play this game?

preceding_turns.speaker: CHRIS PERKINS
preceding_turns.text: Keyleth meteor, what?

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Yeah, that's me.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: As she steps up and emerges from the forest, everyone turns and sees the worry on Vex's face.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: (laughs) Vax pours a drink.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: It was very frightening.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Halt! Who are you?

preceding_turns.speaker: CHRIS PERKINS
preceding_turns.text: My name is Shale.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: What's your business here?

preceding_turns.speaker: CHRIS PERKINS
preceding_turns.text: I was following a scouting party out to the mountain. But these old bones don't take me as far as fast as they used to.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Do I recognize Shale?

speaker: MATT
raw_text: Yeah, three hours.

preceding_turns.speaker: ASHLEY
preceding_turns.text: Wait, what did you just say? Oh.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: We knew that.

preceding_turns.speaker: ASHLEY
preceding_turns.text: Yes. Realized it as I was saying it.

preceding_turns.speaker: CHRIS PERKINS
preceding_turns.text: That is how I wanted to die. Fighting the dragon.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: It's funny, we almost just died fighting the dragon. How ironic.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: We put a good hurting on it, yeah. Do you-- I mean, not like I'm questioning what can you do, because I don't think it's very much.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: I'm actually great. I'm just in a smoldering meteor and look like a badass.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Which caught your attention, the large, crashing boom.

preceding_turns.speaker: CHRIS PERKINS
preceding_turns.text: That was it.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: I yank on Vex's hand from down below and say, pick me up! Put me on your shoulder!

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: What's the matter? Can you not see anything down there? I pop Trinket out and put Vax on top of him. Don't you feel so tall now?

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: I am tall.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: How much longer is he going to be tiny?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: How long does the potion last?

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: I rolled a three, so that's three hours.

speaker: MATT
raw_text: Since you guys landed here? It's probably been about two, three minutes.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Well, we can obviously fly.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: There are sparse trees, most of them dead or dried out towards the top, but other than that the vegetation is pretty sparse.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Look, I'm a little jacked up. I think those two are a little jacked up.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: I could use a short rest.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: It's fair to assume that he will do the same thing, yeah?

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Yeah.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Go brush his teeth, have a deuce, all that stuff.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: We're trying to catch the dragon while he's still debilitated, right?

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: The longer we wait, the harder it will be.

preceding_turns.speaker: ASHLEY
preceding_turns.text: What if I just heal everybody and we keep on?

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Yeah, that's great.

preceding_turns.speaker: ASHLEY
preceding_turns.text: I don't want to lose the damage that's on him.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Yeah, heal us and let's keep going. Quickly. How long has it been, a minute? Two minutes?

speaker: MATT
raw_text: Since you guys landed here? It's probably been about two, three minutes.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Well, we can obviously fly.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: There are sparse trees, most of them dead or dried out towards the top, but other than that the vegetation is pretty sparse.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Look, I'm a little jacked up. I think those two are a little jacked up.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: I could use a short rest.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: It's fair to assume that he will do the same thing, yeah?

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Yeah.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Go brush his teeth, have a deuce, all that stuff.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: We're trying to catch the dragon while he's still debilitated, right?

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: The longer we wait, the harder it will be.

preceding_turns.speaker: ASHLEY
preceding_turns.text: What if I just heal everybody and we keep on?

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Yeah, that's great.

preceding_turns.speaker: ASHLEY
preceding_turns.text: I don't want to lose the damage that's on him.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Yeah, heal us and let's keep going. Quickly. How long has it been, a minute? Two minutes?

speaker: MATT
raw_text: The broom is slower than the eagles. Eagle speed you're looking at about-- I'm estimating somewhere between ten to 20 minutes.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Look, I'm a little jacked up. I think those two are a little jacked up.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: I could use a short rest.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: It's fair to assume that he will do the same thing, yeah?

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Yeah.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Go brush his teeth, have a deuce, all that stuff.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: We're trying to catch the dragon while he's still debilitated, right?

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: The longer we wait, the harder it will be.

preceding_turns.speaker: ASHLEY
preceding_turns.text: What if I just heal everybody and we keep on?

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Yeah, that's great.

preceding_turns.speaker: ASHLEY
preceding_turns.text: I don't want to lose the damage that's on him.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Yeah, heal us and let's keep going. Quickly. How long has it been, a minute? Two minutes?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Since you guys landed here? It's probably been about two, three minutes.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: We've got to move.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Can we travel a mile up a mountain quickly?

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: We have eagles.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Even at your fastest pace, eagle speed...

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: The broom and the two eagles at this point?

speaker: MATT
raw_text: The Scrying will take you about ten minutes or so.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Should we mist form it there? Can we do that?

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: That takes too long to think about.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: I can get us there via trees.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: But you'd need to know a tree that's there.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: I need to know a tree that's there. I could Scry, look for the closest tree, teleport us there, but by the time I'm getting there, I'm exhausted.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Are you able to see a tree from a great distance?

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: If I Scry.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Not even by eyesight?

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: I mean, I guess I can look around and take a guess.

preceding_turns.speaker: ASHLEY
preceding_turns.text: I'm going to cast Mass Cure Wounds on everybody.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: All right. Go ahead and do that. And Shale, you feel nice and warm at full health as the Mass Cure Wounds tickles you.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Is your arm back?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Unfortunately, no. If only!

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: The temporary hit points never go back up, right?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Correct, no. They were just for that little bit of damage.

preceding_turns.speaker: CHRIS PERKINS
preceding_turns.text: Lost it in an avalanche, I did.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Today?

preceding_turns.speaker: CHRIS PERKINS
preceding_turns.text: No, turd.

speaker: MATT
raw_text: To attune to it will take you a short rest.

preceding_turns.speaker: CHRIS PERKINS
preceding_turns.text: You take your rest, love. I'll start up the mountain.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Shale walks away and starts hiking up the rocks at the very base of Gatshadow.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Can I just say that we agree that the reason we didn't do what Zanror wanted and go to the lair is because it's a fucking death trap?

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Yes, but the dragon is injured now.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: But he's going to heal up by the time we get there.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Not all the way.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Pretty good.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: It's still the best chance we have. We have to kill it now.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: In its lair?

preceding_turns.speaker: CHRIS PERKINS
preceding_turns.text: It's your destiny to fight the dragon, Grog.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Wait, what? What was that, Shale? Is that true?

preceding_turns.speaker: CHRIS PERKINS
preceding_turns.text: No, it's not true. (laughter)

preceding_turns.speaker: ASHLEY
preceding_turns.text: Everybody is healed 27.

preceding_turns.speaker: CHRIS PERKINS
preceding_turns.text: It's just a thing old women say.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Just let him believe it.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Kiki, may I have my-- Sorry. (higher voice) May I have my cloak back?

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Oh, yeah, sure.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Thank you. (flump, whoosh)

speaker: MATT
raw_text: Short rest or not, what are you guys doing?

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: So are we resting?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: That's up to you guys.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: I don't know. But I'm still putting it on.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: When I turned into the eagle, what happened to the Bloodaxe and chain?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Actually, you check, and the equipment that you have attuned to you and attached go into the form with you, so at this moment you realize, "Oh, the axe!" You look behind you, and it's just been dragging behind you in the dirt, carving this path in the rocks and rubble.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Yeah. That's nifty.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Like a kid with a deflated balloon you're just dragging behind you.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Can I-- well, I can't sense anything about it with Hunter's Mark, I just know where he is.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: You know where it is.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: And he's up in his lair.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: He is up at the top of Gatshadow, somewhere in that mountain.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Moving around, or is he chilling?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: At the moment, seems chilling.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Let's go.

speaker: MATT
raw_text: It's about a mile up. You're looking at the better part of a day.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: When I turned into the eagle, what happened to the Bloodaxe and chain?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Actually, you check, and the equipment that you have attuned to you and attached go into the form with you, so at this moment you realize, "Oh, the axe!" You look behind you, and it's just been dragging behind you in the dirt, carving this path in the rocks and rubble.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Yeah. That's nifty.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Like a kid with a deflated balloon you're just dragging behind you.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Can I-- well, I can't sense anything about it with Hunter's Mark, I just know where he is.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: You know where it is.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: And he's up in his lair.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: He is up at the top of Gatshadow, somewhere in that mountain.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Moving around, or is he chilling?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: At the moment, seems chilling.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Let's go.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Short rest or not, what are you guys doing?

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: How long would the hike up the mountain be? An hour?

speaker: MATT
raw_text: You're still looking at about an hour and a half at that point, with people moving there and back and getting enough rest. You can't short rest on the trip, and the short rest is about an hour, half an hour.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: You know where it is.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: And he's up in his lair.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: He is up at the top of Gatshadow, somewhere in that mountain.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Moving around, or is he chilling?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: At the moment, seems chilling.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Let's go.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Short rest or not, what are you guys doing?

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: How long would the hike up the mountain be? An hour?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: It's about a mile up. You're looking at the better part of a day.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Oh, a mile up.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Yeah.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Unless we fly.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: What if we take shifts with the eagles?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Take shifts with the eagles? Back and forth?

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: Eagles fly a group up, fly down, pick up another group and bring them?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Probably about an hour.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: But that's an hour with a short rest for everybody as well.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: If you're going to do it that way, yeah.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Half could rest at the top while the eagles fly back, and the ones at the bottom could rest.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: So there's a short rest.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Shifts of resting, yeah.

speaker: MATT
raw_text: Right, but you still have to have the eagles that went to the bottom to pick up the second team to get there, so you're still looking at about an hour and a half to two hours.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Oh, a mile up.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Yeah.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Unless we fly.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: What if we take shifts with the eagles?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Take shifts with the eagles? Back and forth?

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: Eagles fly a group up, fly down, pick up another group and bring them?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Probably about an hour.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: But that's an hour with a short rest for everybody as well.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: If you're going to do it that way, yeah.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Half could rest at the top while the eagles fly back, and the ones at the bottom could rest.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: So there's a short rest.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Shifts of resting, yeah.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: You're still looking at about an hour and a half at that point, with people moving there and back and getting enough rest. You can't short rest on the trip, and the short rest is about an hour, half an hour.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Eagles drop off, they rest while the eagles return, and the people at the bottom rest while the eagles are making the trip, no?

speaker: MATT
raw_text: You have about another 40 minutes of the eagles.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Eagles drop off, they rest while the eagles return, and the people at the bottom rest while the eagles are making the trip, no?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Right, but you still have to have the eagles that went to the bottom to pick up the second team to get there, so you're still looking at about an hour and a half to two hours.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: I could also use another spell and summon a couple more eagles.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Sure.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: I could summon two more eagles.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: That's worth it.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: And that could get how many of us up?

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: All of us up at the same time.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: But one is just for Grog, right?

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: But two more eagles would get all of us up?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: It's a concentration spell. You can only have one of it up at a time.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: It is?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Yeah. And it only lasts for an hour, and you've already been at this for probably about ten, 15 minutes.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: So they're about to go away.

speaker: MATT
raw_text: Okay. I'll let it happen. Usually, you attune one item in a short rest.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: I don't know what that means.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: We'll find out. I'd like to re-attune to the Cloak of Displacement. And the Boots of Haste, are they spent for the day for anybody? If I were to wear them, could I get the juice or no? They're done?

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: They're too big for you, teeny.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: They would shrink to me. I'm going to re-attune my Keen Dagger, then, for the moment. So it's Deathwalkers Ward, Keen Dagger, Cloak. Three items.

speaker: MATT
raw_text: Yeah. For the next attack, it's a 19. Misses you? 20? Yeah. The second claw swings through, at this point, from the pain of the first strike of the dragon claw hits you, you back up to the very edge of the cave and duck out of the way as you hear the wind change. As you do, you hear the stone crash behind you as the claw misses you, slamming instead to the side of the cavern wall. It's now your turn.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: No, you've still got Uncanny Dodge on one of the attacks. Just from your roguish instincts.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Displacer Cloak.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Correct. It gets disadvantage on the first attack, which is going to be a bite. That's a natural 19, and a two! With the two, that brings it to a 17.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Nope.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: So with that, you (whoosh) see for a split second, the ripple of teeth snap before you, missing your face by mere inches before they disappear once again. Two more claws (whoosh) towards you. The first one, that's a 19 again and a seven. That's going to be a 22.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Just hits. Uncanny Dodge.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Oh, your AC would be 20 because you're not hasted right now.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Oh, you're right. Thank you.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: So that would put you at 18 points of slashing damage.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Halved to nine.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Halved to nine. Also your displacement is gone from your cloak.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: For this round?

speaker: MATT
raw_text: So with that, the third shot (click), nothing emerges from it. And you look down. It's not been a day for Percy.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: So you're firing in this direction?

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: Yep.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Go ahead and make your attacks at disadvantage.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: Disadvantage. That is a 25.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Due to the massive size of this currently still invisible entity, you do manage to catch an odd bullet towards its form. Go ahead and roll damage.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: Oh my god. Ten points of piercing damage.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: All righty. (gunshot sound) Gun goes off, echoes, little bit of dust comes loose from the ceiling and falls down as you ready for your next shot.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: I'll take the next shot. Forget it. Might as well go for it.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: At disadvantage.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: That's 20.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: This time, the shot goes the second time (gunshot sound) poofs against some stone in the distance, not hitting its mark.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: Third shot for the run. Fucking--

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Natural 20.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: And a one.

speaker: MATT
raw_text: And that's how you end a round. Pike, you're up.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: You can't see anything. But you do hear right in front of you a growling, snarling bear. You see elements and flashes of the bear that you saw Vex summon forth from her throat.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: And the dagger disappeared into something, right?

preceding_turns.speaker: CHRIS PERKINS
preceding_turns.text: All right. But I can't hear the dragon? At all? It's stomping around.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: It's stomping around a little bit. You saw where the actual projectile came from, so you know it's somewhere in this vicinity.

preceding_turns.speaker: CHRIS PERKINS
preceding_turns.text: Well, you're not very brave, are you? Invisible! Hiding! Afraid to show your face? Taunt it. Oh, bollocks! (laughter)

preceding_turns.speaker: CHRIS PERKINS
preceding_turns.text: Well, that would be a nine.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: A nine. You hear, slowly curling out from the darkness ahead of you, "Bravery means nothing. Survival and victory mean everything!"

preceding_turns.speaker: CHRIS PERKINS
preceding_turns.text: Oh, shut up, you flying suitcase!

speaker: MATT
raw_text: I mean, that will give you a little more action, you just can't attack from the broom because you'll have disadvantage on your attacks since you haven't finished your set-up yet.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Okay. As part of your attack action? Is that what you're doing?

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Yeah, I'm going to have Trinket do one of his attacks so I can move and run.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: As your bonus action on it? Okay, go for it. Have Trinket roll for an attack. With disadvantage because it is invisible.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: That doesn't count? I feel like it still counts, though. It's only 15.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: 15. Trinket slashes out in the air. With the obfuscation of the Stinking Cloud and the invisible dragon, Trinket's just getting mouthfuls of air.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: What does Stinking Cloud do to me?

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Makes you sick.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Fuck. I try to run away. I try to run around and run past Umbrasyl.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: You're not in the cloud.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: But I would go through it if I tried to run between its legs. I'll just try to run along to the side.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: On the broom, still?

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Sure! Why the fuck not?! I'm on the broom. Does it matter?

speaker: MATT
raw_text: Hold on, getting there. We're getting there. It's getting math crunchy here, guys. So 24 plus nine, so that's 33, plus-- the necrotic?

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: I keep rolling, yeah?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Well, do the damage for the critical, yeah.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: This and the next round are going to be really ugly.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Kill it. Fucking kill it!

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: 24. Okay.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Is the roll and then, what? Double it or add another--?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: The dice roll is? Well, you roll 1d12. What was that, the first d12 you rolled?

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: That was a 15. Oh, the dice roll? 12.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: 12. So make a-- it was a 12?

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Yeah.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Oh, shit! Okay, that's a 24. And then you add your modifier. And then roll two more d12s. So, that's 24 plus, is that seven?

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Yeah.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Plus the two for the axe. Oh my god! That'll be at 33. Plus the necrotic-- oh, fuck! And that's doubled too.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: So that's an 11 for the second d12.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Okay.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Should we write these numbers down?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Yeah, we have to now. So it's 24 plus two for the axe, plus seven for the strength.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Plus five for the necrotic--

speaker: MATT
raw_text: Amazing what a dragon can do in two hours.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: As you unload on Umbrasyl, the pent-up anger and frustration, you start slashing over and over again. You guys watch as the blade shines in the air in the presence of its naturally designed enemy. You see at the corners of his outfit little tufts of dark smoke beginning to appear. The scar of Orthax still shows when the fury builds within Percy. As each blow strikes, cutting away hunks and bits of dragon flesh and scale, Umbrasyl is heavily wounded and looking desperate. That ends your turn, I believe?

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Oh my god, he's still alive?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: You guys gave it time to rest.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: Fuck it, bonus action, I'm putting on the mask and standing my ground. Fuck it.

speaker: MATT
raw_text: (sizzle) I can make that joke in two weeks! As you go and bring Trinket around for a second, using the last bit of the magic that's been afforded to you as a ranger, Trinket comes to. (bear grunt) A lot of the fur has been singed off, and spotted flesh with acid burn, but is conscious and alive, and weak, Trinket leans forward as you're cradling his head and licks the side of your face and tucks his nose into the side of your shoulder.

preceding_turns.speaker: ASHLEY
preceding_turns.text: I run over to Percy.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: I run over to Trinket.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: I drop my water elemental form.

preceding_turns.speaker: ASHLEY
preceding_turns.text: I touch Percy and I cast Heal on him.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Nice.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: I cast Cure Wounds on Trinket.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: As you stand there, Shale, watching the dragon fall as Grog Strongjaw, the once runt of the Herd of Storms, slams the blade over and over again in the dragon's head, a little part of you is a little jealous, and another part proud, and a little sad that this was not the time for you to lose your life in battle against a mighty dragon.

preceding_turns.speaker: ASHLEY
preceding_turns.text: Oh, Shale.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Can you believe it?

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Shale jumps into acid.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Just walks in.

preceding_turns.speaker: CHRIS PERKINS
preceding_turns.text: Dissolves.

speaker: MATT
raw_text: (bear grunt) You go ahead and help Percy get off the ground. You come to consciousness again and you see Pike's hand touching yours as she lifts you. Suddenly, a surge of invigoration hits your body, and you find yourself feeling a lot better than you did a few moments ago.

preceding_turns.speaker: ASHLEY
preceding_turns.text: Oh, Shale.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Can you believe it?

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Shale jumps into acid.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Just walks in.

preceding_turns.speaker: CHRIS PERKINS
preceding_turns.text: Dissolves.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: (sizzle) I can make that joke in two weeks! As you go and bring Trinket around for a second, using the last bit of the magic that's been afforded to you as a ranger, Trinket comes to. (bear grunt) A lot of the fur has been singed off, and spotted flesh with acid burn, but is conscious and alive, and weak, Trinket leans forward as you're cradling his head and licks the side of your face and tucks his nose into the side of your shoulder.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: You were so brave in here!

speaker: MATT
raw_text: You have, probably another 20, 30 minutes or so before it fades.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: There were still a few members of the Herd still alive. A good half dozen.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Zanror's baby mama is still preggo.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Yeah, exactly.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: It's also very lean times in Westruun. Most of this gold should go back to Westruun.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Most of it.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Did we contribute any of our own gold into this pile?

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: That's back in Westruun.

preceding_turns.speaker: CHRIS PERKINS
preceding_turns.text: I can return with the remnants of the Herd and gather up whatever you don't take.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: And bring it back to Westruun.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: We'll divvy it up later.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Let's divvy it up.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: If we take most of the gold that came from Westruun, we're a big pack of assholes.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: I said we weren't going to! We're going to take it back to Westruun.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: You're a tiny pack of assholes right now.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Is he still tiny? He's still tiny.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Three hours.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Probably another two hours.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: It's only been an hour? No.

speaker: MATT
raw_text: All right. So as you guys are gathering the elements of this dragon's hoard, gathering the equipment, the art pieces, the relics, all things that were part of this-- and it is, especially since you know, as far as dragon hoards go, especially for an ancient dragon, it isn't huge. And with the recent moving in, you get the idea that this dragon's true hoard probably exists elsewhere. This was either a temporary lair, or was intended to be the new lair, and the process of transitioning hadn't quite begun. Most of the gold here seems to have been what was given to Umbrasyl by the Herd once they had taken over Westruun.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: We need Fassbender.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Keyleth, you're going to butcher this dragon, right?

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Yes. 100 percent. I start going to town on it.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Because this is going to be worth a lot of fucking money.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Yeah. Percy, do you have any extra vials? Empty vials for blood?

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: We should get some of the blood and put it in the Bag of Colding.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Hey, Percy! So glad you're alive and not burning in acid. Do you have any extra vials?

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: Do I?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Checking in your various pieces of equipment you have on you, as opposed to what you have stored elsewhere, you had a few vials. They are a little jangly when you reach for them, and you realize they've been crushed.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: We have some empty potion bottles.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: I hand mine over from the one I gave Vex.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: The one I used as well.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Go ahead and make a nature check.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: I'll take that 16 for a 27. Nature check, yeah? 27.

speaker: MATT
raw_text: That you saw in Krieg's lair? No. You do know, from having one made by Allura within your keep before, that they take quite some time to ingrain. Dragon's only really had over a week or so. So, gathering up the materials, the remnants of that hoard, you guys descend down the mountain if you wish, or is there anything else you wish to accomplish within the actual lair?

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Maybe he has to give it to Thordak in a dragon pyramid scheme.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: We don't find any of those glyphs like there were in--

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: We already looked for those.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Not the circles, I meant the transportation glyph that we beamed into his--

speaker: MATT
raw_text: "Good. We'll set out tomorrow. At dawn, we plan."

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "It is. But there's been a lot of cost in it, too."

preceding_turns.speaker: CHRIS PERKINS
preceding_turns.text: There's always cost. We will all die, it just depends on how. We died fighting a dragon, and we lived fighting a dragon. What more do you want? That's the smile I want to see.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "I'm glad you're back, Shale. Your wisdom stays strong."

preceding_turns.speaker: CHRIS PERKINS
preceding_turns.text: Well, now that Kevdak's out of the way, maybe people will listen to what I have to say for a change.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "We've been talking here. I think the Herd of Storms died in battle with the dragon. There's not many of us left."

preceding_turns.speaker: CHRIS PERKINS
preceding_turns.text: But there are other herds.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "There are. And while we lost the Rivermaw, I think maybe it's time we went and joined them."

preceding_turns.speaker: CHRIS PERKINS
preceding_turns.text: I like that idea.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "They're damper lands. Better for raising children. Take a break from all this warring and go back to a simple life. Will you come with us, Shale?"

preceding_turns.speaker: CHRIS PERKINS
preceding_turns.text: Of course.
